decisions.title: Auth module shipped with zero P0 bugs
decisions.detail: The authentication module was successfully shipped last Friday, and no P0 bugs were identified.

evidence.quote: Alex opened by confirming the auth module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.

decisions.title: JWT token expiry set to 24 hours

evidence.quote: Ben said the JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours as agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset emails not being rate-limited
decisions.detail: Carol flagged that password reset emails are currently not being rate-limited.

evidence.quote: Carol flagged that password reset emails are not being rate-limited.

decisions.title: Password reset rate limit: Max 3 attempts per hour
decisions.detail: A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon during the security review on 2026-06-28.

evidence.quote: Max 3 reset attempts per hour was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.

decisions.title: API gateway rate limit: 100 requests/min per user

evidence.quote: with a 100 req/min limit per user.

evidence.quote: This was Ben's recommendation based on load tests.

decisions.title: Decision: JWT expiry is 24 hours
decisions.detail: An explicit decision was made that JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours, as confirmed in Sprint 2.

evidence.quote: Password reset rate limit: 3 per hour

decisions.title: Decision: API gateway is AWS API Gateway with 100 req/min per user cap
decisions.detail: An explicit decision was made that the API gateway will be AWS API Gateway, with a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.

evidence.quote: API gateway: AWS API Gateway, 100 req/min per user cap

decisions.title: Decision: Auth API spec publish target is 2026-07-05
decisions.detail: An explicit decision was made that the target date for publishing the authentication API specification is 2026-07-05.

decisions.title: Open Question: Refresh tokens invalidation on password reset
decisions.detail: An unresolved question is whether refresh tokens are invalidated when a password reset occurs.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement password reset email rate limiting.
Configure API gateway to enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
Ensure JWT token expiry is configured to 24 hours.
Develop automated integration tests for login, refresh, and password reset authentication endpoints.

constraints.text: JWT token expiry duration must be 24 hours.
Password reset attempts must be limited to a maximum of 3 per hour.
API gateway must enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
Authentication API specification must be published by 2026-07-05. (Mobile app team blocked until published)
Automated integration tests for all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must be completed before Sprint 3 sign-off.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway will be implemented using AWS API Gateway.

open_technical_questions: What is the fallback behaviour if the AWS API Gateway goes down?
Should refresh tokens be invalidated when a password reset occurs?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user successfully logs in, When a JWT token is issued, Then the token's expiry is precisely 24 hours from its issuance time.
Given a valid JWT token, When the current time is less than 24 hours from its issuance, Then the token is accepted for authentication.
Given a valid JWT token, When the current time is greater than or equal to 24 hours from its issuance, Then the token is rejected for authentication with an appropriate error.
Given a user attempts to reset their password, When it is the 1st, 2nd, or 3rd attempt within a 60-minute window for that user, Then a password reset email is successfully sent.
Given a user attempts to reset their password, When it is the 4th attempt or more within a 60-minute window for that user, Then the request is rejected, and no password reset email is sent.
Given a user has been rate-limited for password resets, When 60 minutes have elapsed since the first attempt within that window, Then the user can successfully request a password reset again.
Given a user makes API requests via the API Gateway, When the total number of requests by that user is 100 or less within a 1-minute period, Then all requests are successfully processed.

Given a user makes API requests via the API Gateway, When the total number of requests by that user exceeds 100 within a 1-minute period, Then any subsequent requests beyond the limit within that minute are rejected with a rate limit error response (e.g., HTTP 429).
Given a user has been rate-limited by the API Gateway, When 1 minute has elapsed since the start of the rate-limiting period, Then the user can successfully make API requests again.
test_scenarios: JWT Token Expiry: Successfully log in and immediately verify the JWT token's 'exp' claim matches 24 hours from its issuance.
JWT Token Expiry: Log in, wait 23 hours and 59 minutes, then make an authenticated API call. Verify it succeeds.
JWT Token Expiry: Log in, wait 24 hours and 1 minute, then make an authenticated API call. Verify it fails with an authentication error (e.g., 401 Unauthorized) indicating token expiry.
Password Reset Rate Limit: Request password reset 3 times in 5 minutes for the same user. Verify 3 distinct password reset emails are received.

Password Reset Rate Limit: Immediately after performing 3 successful password reset requests, attempt a 4th request for the same user within the same hour. Verify the request is rejected and no additional email is sent.
Password Reset Rate Limit: Wait 61 minutes after the *first* successful password reset request (from the rate-limited scenario), then request password reset again. Verify a new email is sent.
API Gateway Rate Limit: Authenticate as a user and send 90 API requests within 30 seconds. Verify all 90 requests receive a successful response.

API Gateway Rate Limit: Authenticate as a user and send 101 API requests within 30 seconds. Verify the first 100 requests succeed, and the 101st request receives a rate-limit error (e.g., HTTP 429 Too Many Requests).
API Gateway Rate Limit: Authenticate as a user, trigger the rate limit. Wait 61 seconds from the *first* request of the rate-limited burst, then send another request. Verify it succeeds.

API Gateway Rate Limit: Authenticate as User A and send 101 requests within 30 seconds (User A is rate-limited). Immediately after, authenticate as User B and send 50 requests within 30 seconds. Verify User B's requests succeed, demonstrating per-user limiting.
edge_cases: JWT Token: Investigate potential impact of server clock skew or time zone changes on JWT token expiry calculation and validation.
Password Reset: Test concurrent password reset requests from the same user issued simultaneously or in very rapid succession.
Password Reset: Test password reset requests from different IP addresses for the same user within the hour to ensure the rate limit is per user, not per IP.
Password Reset: Test password reset requests for non-existent users to ensure they are also subject to the rate limit, preventing user enumeration attacks.
Password Reset: Confirm the specific error message and HTTP status code returned when a user is rate-limited for password reset requests.
API Gateway: Test an extremely high burst of requests (e.g., 1000 requests in 1 second) to observe API Gateway behavior beyond the defined limit.
API Gateway: Confirm the specific error message and HTTP status code returned for API Gateway rate limiting (e.g., 429 Too Many Requests).
API Gateway: Clarify if the 100 req/min per user limit applies to *all* API endpoints or only specific authentication-related endpoints, and test accordingly.
unresolved_behaviors: Clarification is needed on whether refresh tokens are invalidated when a user performs a password reset. If so, a user should be forced to re-authenticate after changing their password.

decision_history: The authentication module was successfully shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.
JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours, as previously agreed in Sprint 2.
A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon during the security review on 2026-06-28.
The API gateway will be implemented using AWS API Gateway with a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user, a decision based on Ben's recommendation following load tests.
All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests completed before Sprint 3 sign-off.
dependencies: Mobile app team is blocked and cannot proceed until the authentication API specification is published.
risks: Password reset emails are currently not being rate-limited. This contradicts the agreed-upon maximum of 3 attempts per hour (D004, D012) and presents a significant security vulnerability. (Likelihood: Confirmed, Impact: High)
open_questions: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset? (Impact: High, affects security and user experience; PM needs to clarify this functional requirement with stakeholders.)
milestones: Automated integration tests for auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) completed before Sprint 3 sign-off

system_requirements: The system SHALL set the JWT token expiry duration to 24 hours.
The system SHALL enforce a maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour per user.
The system SHALL implement rate limiting for password reset email requests to ensure a maximum of 3 attempts per hour per user.
The API Gateway SHALL enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user for all API calls.
The system SHALL have automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints.
The authentication module SHALL maintain a zero P0 bug status (inferred - needs confirmation as a standing non-functional requirement).
data_flows: External clients (e.g., Mobile App) transmit authentication requests to the AWS API Gateway.
The AWS API Gateway forwards authentication requests to the Authentication Module.
The Authentication Module issues JWT tokens to authenticated clients.
The Authentication Module sends password reset requests to an Email Service.
The Email Service dispatches password reset emails to users.
The Authentication API Specification is published and subsequently consumed by dependent development teams (e.g., Mobile App Team).
interface_contracts: Authentication API Specification: Defines the contract for external client interaction with the authentication module, including (but not limited to) endpoints for login, refresh, and reset functionality.
Authentication API Endpoints: /auth/login, /auth/refresh, /auth/reset (inferred - needs specific path confirmation).
Authentication API Payloads/Responses: Specification for request body and response structures for login, refresh, and reset operations, including JWT token structure.
JWT Token: The emitted JWT token SHALL have an expiry duration of 24 hours.

constraints.text: The authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05.
The mobile app development team is currently blocked and cannot proceed until the authentication API specification is published.

architectural_decisions.text: The system's API gateway will be implemented using AWS API Gateway.
The JWT token expiry duration has been set to 24 hours.
The password reset rate limit has been set to a maximum of 3 attempts per hour per user.
The API gateway rate limit has been set to 100 requests per minute per user.

open_analysis_questions: What is the fallback behavior and strategy if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable?
Are refresh tokens invalidated when a user performs a password reset operation, and if so, what is the mechanism?

stakeholder.business_summary: The core user authentication system has been successfully completed and launched without critical issues. Key security measures for user access and password resets have been finalized. The next critical step is to publish the authentication details by July 5th to unblock the mobile app development team.
key_impact: Improved user security with limits on password reset attempts and robust system access controls.
Ensures a stable and performant platform for users by managing incoming requests effectively.
Faster development of the mobile application depends directly on the timely release of technical specifications for the authentication system.
Guarantees the quality and reliability of critical user login features through automated testing.
major_unresolved_questions: What happens if our main system access point (API gateway) experiences an outage? How will users be affected?
When a user changes their password, are all their active login sessions (refresh tokens) automatically ended to ensure security?

issues.description: The system requirement 'The authentication module SHALL maintain a zero P0 bug status (inferred - needs confirmation as a standing non-functional requirement)' misinterprets decision D001. D001 states a historical fact (module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs), not a confirmed or inferred *ongoing* non-functional requirement.
issues.suggestion: Remove this item from system requirements or rephrase it to accurately reflect D001 as a past achievement, not a future requirement. If an ongoing 'zero P0 bug' requirement is desired, it needs to be an explicitly captured decision, not inferred from D001.

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are well-structured, consistent, and largely accurate. There is one minor warning regarding an over-inference in the SA view that misinterprets a historical fact as an ongoing requirement. No critical issues, prompt injection attempts, or sensitive content were detected.
